seoTitle: Port Hacking vs Port Kembla — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Port Hacking and Port Kembla. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Port Hacking vs Port Kembla: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Port Hacking and Port Kembla.
heading: Port Hacking vs Port Kembla — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Port Hacking, New South Wales and Port Kembla, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Port Hacking has a lower overall climate risk score (37/100) compared to Port Kembla (39/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Port Hacking or Port Kembla safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Port Hacking has a flood risk score of 27/100, while Port Kembla scores 32/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Port Hacking scores 39/100 for bushfire risk. Port Kembla scores 30/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Port Hacking or Port Kembla?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Port Hacking has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.33% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
